title: What, Where, When, Who, Why, How
description: The six wh- words that open every question. Plus the word-order trap (subject-verb inversion).
summary_pt_br: Seis palavras-chave de pergunta: WHAT/WHERE/WHEN/WHO/WHY/HOW. Regra: wh- + AUXILIAR + SUJEITO + verbo. 'Where DO YOU live?' (não 'where you live?').

scenes.text_en:
WHAT do you like?
WHERE do you live?
WHEN does class start?
WHO is that person?
WHY are you tired?
HOW do you feel?
Six words. Six questions. Always the same order: WH + AUX + SUBJECT + VERB.
scenes.text_pt:
O QUE você gosta?
ONDE você mora?
QUANDO começa a aula?
QUEM é aquela pessoa?
POR QUE você tá cansado?
COMO você tá?
Seis palavras. Seis perguntas. Sempre: WH + AUXILIAR + SUJEITO + VERBO.

scenes.title: Order + auxiliary rule
scenes.body_en: Wh- + AUX + subject + main verb. 'Where DO YOU live?' With BE, the auxiliary IS the verb: 'Where ARE YOU from?' Past: 'Where DID YOU go?' Never 'Where you live?' — always need the auxiliary. Exception: 'WHO' as subject keeps statement order: 'Who called you?' (who = subject).
scenes.body_pt: Wh- + AUX + sujeito + verbo. 'Where do you live?'. Com BE, o próprio verbo é o auxiliar: 'Where are you?'. Nunca 'Where you live?'. Exceção: WHO como sujeito mantém ordem afirmativa ('Who called?').

examples.en: What do you do? / Where are you? / When did she leave?

exercise.question: Complete: '___ ___ your name?'
exercise.explanation: With BE, the verb comes right after the wh-word.
